_comment: Follow-up log read by .claude/skills/health-review/SKILL.md as part of the layer-2 review. Each entry tracks something a prior review surfaced that wasn't fully resolved at the time, with a trigger condition the next /health-review run must evaluate. See SKILL.md § 'Mandatory pre-review step' for how this list is consumed. Schema documented at comms/schema/follow_ups.schema.json.

follow_ups.title: Verify bybit_2 circuit breaker fires on next 170131 cluster
follow_ups.description: PR #741 added _EXCHANGE_REJECTION_PAUSE_THRESHOLD=3 in src/core/coordinator.py. The breaker auto-flips an account to dry_run via set_account_dry_run after 3 consecutive exchange_rejected. The 2026-05-10 review observed a 34% rejection rate (20/58 trades) with no backoff; tests pin the contract but the live behaviour has not yet been observed.
follow_ups.expected_check: If the diag tail in this review window contains any trade with status='rejected' on bybit_2, count consecutive rejections. After ≥3 consecutive, the next dispatch should appear as a dry-run (no exchange call). Cross-reference src/units/accounts._DRY_RUN_OVERRIDES via /api/diag/snapshot — bybit_2.dry_run should flip to True. A critical-level alert in runtime_logs should reference 'auto-paused after N consecutive exchange rejections'.
follow_ups.trigger_condition: Any trade in the 6h review window has status='rejected' on bybit_2.
follow_ups.resolution_criteria: Close after a single observed cluster where the breaker fired correctly, OR after 30 days with no rejections (pin closure to absence-of-evidence).
follow_ups.closed_reason: OBE — PR 3 (#781) migrated bybit_2 from Bybit V5 spot-margin to USDT-margined linear perpetuals at 3× leverage. The 170131 ErrCode is a spot-margin-specific Bybit response; the linear perp path validates against walletBalance directly with no borrow-capacity intermediary. No account in production is on spot-margin post-cutover, so this follow-up cannot trigger. Re-open with a fresh ID if a spot-margin account is ever reintroduced. Note: the 2026-05-10 root-cause analysis confirmed the original '20/58 rejected' cluster was actually INTERNAL sized_qty=0 refusals (the LTV fallback path collapsing capacity when Bybit's availableToBorrow flickered), not exchange-side 170131 rejections — see comms/requests/ chat record.

follow_ups.title: RiskManager-vs-Bybit USDT-view mismatch on spot-margin LONG
follow_ups.description: 20/58 bybit_2 spot-margin Buy orders on 2026-05-10 were rejected with Bybit ErrCode 170131 ('Insufficient balance', isLeverage=1) despite RiskManager-approved sizing. The design comment at src/units/accounts/execute.py:746-751 says RiskManager owns sizing for spot-margin from USDT collateral; the live evidence shows RiskManager's USDT view diverges from Bybit's. A LONG-side pre-flight check was deferred from PR #741 because the right semantics are unclear: stale balance read, locked USDT in open orders not subtracted, or actual borrow-pool exhaustion.
follow_ups.expected_check: When the breaker auto-pauses bybit_2 (FU-20260510-001), capture the same wallet snapshot RiskManager used and Bybit's accountInfo + spot-margin borrow capacity at the moment of rejection. Compare. Identify which number the gap is in.
follow_ups.trigger_condition: First observation of FU-20260510-001 firing, OR any future 170131 cluster on a spot-margin account.
follow_ups.resolution_criteria: Close with a follow-up PR adding the pre-flight check OR an explicit decision that the circuit breaker alone is sufficient.
follow_ups.closed_reason: OBE — see FU-20260510-001 closure. The 2026-05-10 follow-up investigation found the original cluster was internal sized_qty=0 refusals (RiskManager refusing because available_usd ≈ collateral × LTV after Bybit returned availableToBorrow=0). Root cause was the spot-margin LTV-fallback path, which no longer fires post-PR 3 since bybit_2 is on linear perps. The architectural class of bug (sizer modelling exchange-side borrow capacity from the client) is gone with the spot-margin path.

follow_ups.title: ETHUSDT silence on turtle_soup despite config
follow_ups.description: config/strategies.yaml configures turtle_soup for BTCUSDT + ETHUSDT, but the 2026-05-10 audit window contained zero ETHUSDT events (orders or eval) over ~75 minutes. Could be a per-symbol cadence mismatch, a market-data fetch failure for ETHUSDT, or the multiplexer dropping the second symbol. Not yet investigated.
follow_ups.expected_check: Filter the audit tail for symbol=='ETHUSDT'. Count turtle_soup_eval events. If the count is zero across the 6h window, raise to anomalies. If non-zero, this follow-up can be closed.
follow_ups.resolution_criteria: Close after a single review where ETHUSDT turtle_soup_eval count > 0, OR after a code change confirms turtle_soup is correctly multi-symbol.
follow_ups.closed_reason: Root cause identified + ETHUSDT dropped from config per operator decision. The per-tick pipeline (src/runtime/pipeline.py:532 turtle_soup_signal_builder, :688 vwap_signal_builder) resolves `symbol` from settings['SYMBOL'] (default BTCUSDT) and never iterates the strategies.yaml `symbols:` list. The list IS read at boot by _symbols_for_account for the leverage pre-flight loop, but per-tick signal generation is single-symbol. So ETHUSDT silence wasn't a fetch failure or multiplexer bug — the strategy was never actually invoked against ETHUSDT. Operator decision 2026-05-11: drop ETHUSDT from turtle_soup config since multi-symbol isn't needed yet. Re-opening would require a real per-symbol multiplexer (one tick per (symbol, strategy), per-symbol audit events, per-symbol sized_qty) — substantive design change, not blocking current operations.

follow_ups.title: scripts/collect_health_snapshot.sh under-reports activity
follow_ups.description: The collector greps *.log for ticks/signals/orders/trades but the live pipeline writes runtime_logs/signal_audit.jsonl (NDJSON, not .log). All snapshots since the format switch have empty TICKS/SIGNALS/ORDERS/TRADES sections even when the bot is actively trading. PR #738 mitigates this by mandating diag-relay pulls in the layer-2 review, but the collector itself is still wrong.
follow_ups.expected_check: Diff snapshot section counts vs. diag-relay counts in the same window. If snapshot reports 'no … in last 1440m' AND diag shows non-zero, the collector is still broken.
follow_ups.trigger_condition: Any /health-review run while the collector script is unmodified.
follow_ups.resolution_criteria: Close once collect_health_snapshot.sh is patched to read signal_audit.jsonl and snapshot/diag counts agree.
follow_ups.closed_reason: Won't-fix-for-now — mitigated by skill runbook. The /health-review skill (.claude/skills/health-review/SKILL.md § 'Mandatory pre-review step') now requires every layer-2 reviewer to pull audit / journal / status / trades via the diag relay BEFORE grading. The collector's per-section under-report is documented in the skill prose as a known limitation and the diag-relay pull is the source of truth. The collector itself produces other useful sections (META, PROCESSES, HEARTBEAT, MONITORING, API, ERRORS, VM) that still work and are not redundant with diag. Patching the collector to also read signal_audit.jsonl would be ~10 lines but introduces a NDJSON-parse step in shell; benefit is marginal given the skill mandates the diag pull. Re-open if a future review surfaces a missed signal due to the under-report.

follow_ups.title: set_leverage still returns retCode=10003 — likely API key permission, not code
follow_ups.description: Re-titled and re-classified 2026-05-11 after extensive evidence gathering. Original hypothesis (PR #782 would fix retCode=10003 by routing through ccxt's private_post_v5_position_set_leverage) is DISPROVEN by direct live observation: at 2026-05-11T14:59:56Z, the deployed code on commit a8d6506 (post-#896, which includes #871/#881/#882) called set_leverage with the V5-correct ccxt method (verified by inspection of src/exchange/bybit_connector.py:111-200) and STILL got retCode=10003 'API key is invalid.' on bybit_2. CRUCIALLY: the SAME client object placed successful linear orders ~5 minutes later (trade_id 784f7e16-..., 092edeea-..., etc.). So the API key is VALID for the order endpoints but INVALID for the position-leverage endpoint. This is almost certainly a Bybit UTA API key permission scope issue: the operator's bybit_2 key has 'Trade > Order' permission enabled but NOT 'Trade > Position' / 'Trade > Set Leverage'. The Bybit UI lets you toggle these independently when issuing/editing a key.
follow_ups.expected_check: OPERATOR-SIDE: log into Bybit UI → Account & Security → API Management → find the bybit_2 key → check 'Permissions'. Confirm 'Trade > Position' / 'Position > Set Leverage' is checked. If not, edit the key to enable it (or re-issue with broader scope). After the key permission flip, restart ict-trader-live and confirm next boot's journalctl shows `set_leverage: BTCUSDT x3 ... retCode=0` or `retCode=110043`.
follow_ups.trigger_condition: Pre-condition is the operator updating the API key permissions in Bybit's UI. Until then, this follow-up will continue to surface retCode=10003 on every boot but won't block trading (order placement is unaffected).
follow_ups.resolution_criteria: Close after one observed boot where set_leverage logs retCode 0 or 110043 AND the live Bybit position reports leverage=3 on bybit_2. Code-side action is exhausted — PR #782 + #871 + #881 + #882 + #896 are all live; this is now exclusively waiting on the API key scope update.

follow_ups.title: Investigate strategy_monocle bypass: two VWAP dispatches on bybit_2 within 3 min
follow_ups.description: Post-PR3-deploy on 2026-05-10, two VWAP dispatches landed on bybit_2 with overlapping open packages: trade_id 2212172585901582336 at 21:12:02 UTC (spot-margin, pre-deploy code, qty=0.003, entry=80673.80) and trade_id 14ae1c19-3814-4494-ab87-65c02b7e11ae at 21:14:45 UTC (linear, post-deploy code, qty=0.006, entry=80654.90). The strategy_monocle gate at src/runtime/pipeline.py::_has_open_package_for_strategy SHOULD have blocked the second dispatch by querying order_packages for status='open' rows under strategy_name='vwap'. It didn't. Operator closed the first position manually post-discovery; the second is running. Possible causes: (a) the 21:12 package row was created at status='dispatched' or similar by the spot-margin path and never transitioned to 'open' before the deploy restarted the trader at 21:13:11; (b) the new linear-path code uses a different default status when inserting the package row; (c) DB read failed silently for the new process at 21:14 and the monocle helper returned None (fail-open); (d) some legitimate reason the open-package gate doesn't apply when the package was inserted by a different process id. Cleanly investigating requires the actual order_packages row history.
follow_ups.expected_check: Pull journal?table=order_packages&limit=20 from the diag relay. Find the two packages by trade_id. Check (a) initial status of each, (b) what status the 21:12 package was in at 21:14:45 (the moment the second dispatch ran), (c) whether linked_trade_id was populated correctly. Also pull journalctl around 21:14 — look for any _has_open_package_for_strategy warning ('DB read failed'). If the bypass cause is identifiable, surface it in recommended_action and propose a fix.
follow_ups.trigger_condition: Any /health-review run (the diag tail covering 2026-05-10 21:00-22:00 still contains the relevant rows for several days).
follow_ups.resolution_criteria: Close with either (a) a fix PR that prevents the same class of bypass, OR (b) an explicit decision that the gap is acceptable post-PR 3 (the cooldown gate from PR 2 #777 may catch similar cases with a different mechanism).
follow_ups.closed_reason: Resolved by evidence — no monocle bypass occurred. journal?table=order_packages pull (issue #810) shows pkg-b43560af1c1f4d66 (the 21:12 spot-margin package, linked_trade_id=1140) was closed by the monitor_reconciler at 21:13:31 UTC with close_reason='stuck_cascade_recovered' and meta reason='linked trade reached terminal status while package stayed open' — i.e. operator manual_closeall on trade 1140 → trade row reached terminal status → reconciler closed the orphaned-open package. By the time the linear dispatch fired at 21:14:44 (pkg-e23467c60e064a25, linked_trade_id=1141) there was no open vwap package on bybit_2; _has_open_package_for_strategy correctly returned False. The handoff was clean: the apparent 'two open packages' was actually a closed-then-reopened sequence mediated by the reconciler. No fix PR required.

follow_ups.title: Validate bybit_2 first 24h on linear perps
follow_ups.description: PR 3 (#781) migrated bybit_2 from spot-margin to USDT-margined linear perpetuals at 3× leverage on 2026-05-10. First linear trade fired at 21:14:45 UTC (trade_id 14ae1c19-3814-4494-ab87-65c02b7e11ae). Two known imperfections at cutover: (a) set_leverage helper bug — actual exchange-side leverage may differ from configured 3× until PR 4 deploys (see FU-005); (b) one orphan spot-margin position from 21:12 that the operator closed manually. The next health-review should confirm the cutover is operating cleanly: trades route through category='linear', qty sizing makes sense given the configured leverage, no orphan reconciler firing on spot-margin positions that no longer exist, no failed_exchange clusters.
follow_ups.expected_check: (1) From journal?table=trades, every bybit_2 trade in the 24h since 2026-05-10T21:14:45+00:00 should have notes/order_id consistent with a linear placement (UUID-style trade_id, not numeric). (2) From journalctl, search for 'category=spot' or 'isLeverage=1' relative to bybit_2 — there should be none after 21:13:11 restart. (3) Spot-margin reconciler logs (_reconcile_orphan_borrows, _reconcile_orphan_positions) should report checked=0 since no spot-margin account exists. If reconciler is checking >0 there's a stale config somewhere. (4) Any failed_exchange cluster on bybit_2 in this window is a regression — surface as concern.
follow_ups.trigger_condition: Any /health-review run in the 7 days following 2026-05-10T21:14:45+00:00.
follow_ups.resolution_criteria: Close after one clean health-review confirming all four checks pass, OR after a regression PR that fixes whatever pattern emerges.
follow_ups.closed_reason: All four checks pass with evidence accumulated 2026-05-11. (1) /api/diag/journal?table=trades pulls during this session showed every bybit_2 trade since 2026-05-10T21:14:45Z has a UUID-style trade_id (e.g. 14ae1c19-..., 092edeea-..., 784f7e16-..., 8d9a91e5-...) — linear placements only, no numeric Bybit V5 order ids. (2) journalctl tails over multiple windows showed no 'category=spot' or 'isLeverage=1' lines for bybit_2 post-restart. (3) PR 5 (#792) deleted the spot-margin reconciler functions entirely, so checked=0 is structural — they cannot fire. (4) No failed_exchange clusters: the only failed_exchange line per boot is the smoke test's deliberate plumbing-on-rejection probe ('NoneType' object has no attribute 'place_order') which is the documented expected outcome. set_leverage retCode=10003 (FU-005) is a separate pre-flight call that does not block order placement; trade dispatch on bybit_2 has been clean across the 17h+ since cutover. Closing 24h window confidently.

follow_ups.title: Unexplained second ict-trader-live restart at 2026-05-10T21:16:35Z
follow_ups.description: The PR 3 deploy at 21:13:11 UTC started ict-trader-live (PID 67273). Three minutes later at ~21:16:35 UTC a second restart occurred and the trader now runs as PID 67504. journalctl shows multiple 'Stopping/Stopped/Started ICT Trader Live' transitions. No deploy or operator-action was fired in that window. Could be (a) a systemd auto-restart after the new code crashed on a tick (check journalctl for a Traceback between 21:13 and 21:16), (b) a separate operator-actions issue I don't have visibility into, (c) some boot_audit or smoke_test path that intentionally restarts. Investigation is bounded — if it was a tick crash, the cause is post-PR3 wiring (e.g. _apply_per_account_leverage raising on the API key bug); if it was external, no action needed.
follow_ups.expected_check: Pull journalctl?unit=ict-trader-live&lines=400 covering 2026-05-10T21:13:00 — 21:17:00 UTC. Look for: (a) any Traceback or 'Tick failed' lines between the two restarts; (b) any 'reboot-vm' / 'restart-bot-service' operator-action issue that fired in that 3 min window. If a Traceback is found, the stacktrace identifies the regression.
follow_ups.trigger_condition: Any /health-review run within 7 days of 2026-05-10. journalctl retention may rotate the relevant lines after that.
follow_ups.resolution_criteria: Close after identifying the cause (tick crash, external restart, or scheduled refresh) and either filing a follow-up PR or marking the cause benign.
follow_ups.closed_reason: Evidence unrecoverable. Attempted retrieval on 2026-05-11 ~07:00 UTC via diag-relay issue #815 (journalctl?unit=ict-trader-live&lines=2000). The reply was capped at GitHub's 55KB comment limit and only reached back ~23 minutes of live-trader log activity (05:22 → 05:45 UTC on 2026-05-11) — the target 2026-05-10 21:13-21:17 UTC window is ~9.5h before the visible window. The journalctl diag endpoint as it stands (max 2000 lines, tail-only) cannot address a 9h-old window at the live-trader's log rate. Re-opening would require either (a) adding ?since=<timestamp> to /api/diag/journalctl, or (b) raising _MAX_JOURNAL_LINES and chunking the response. Tracked as FU-20260511-001.

follow_ups.title: Diag journalctl endpoint can't address historical windows
follow_ups.description: FU-005 and FU-008 closed without evidence because the diag-relay journalctl endpoint is tail-only (-n N, max 2000). At the live-trader's log rate the 2000-line tail reaches back ~20-30 minutes, while time-bounded follow-ups (FU-008 was 7 days, FU-005 needed the first post-PR4 boot) routinely need 8-24h. The 55KB GitHub comment cap further truncates even the tail we DO retrieve. Two viable enhancements: (a) add ?since=<ISO8601> and ?until=<ISO8601> params to the journalctl endpoint, mapping to journalctl --since/--until; (b) chunk responses across multiple issue comments when the JSON exceeds 50KB. Both would also help future incident forensics — any post-mortem more than ~30 min old is currently unreachable from the PM-side session.
follow_ups.expected_check: When a future health-review needs evidence older than ~30 min, observe whether the new endpoint variants allow direct retrieval.
follow_ups.trigger_condition: Any /health-review run that needs to evidence a time-bounded follow-up older than 30 minutes.
follow_ups.resolution_criteria: Close after a PR adds ?since= support to /api/diag/journalctl (and ideally to /api/diag/audit + /api/diag/journal as well, since they share the same 55KB truncation pattern).
follow_ups.closed_reason: Resolved by PR #821 (merged 2026-05-11). /api/diag/journalctl now accepts optional ?since= / ?until= ISO-8601 params and forwards to journalctl's native --since/--until. Strict shape regex (_ISO_TIMESTAMP_RE) blocks injection. The audit + journal endpoints still share the same 55KB truncation pattern; if a future review needs historical-window pulls from those, open a fresh follow-up to extend the same param family there. FU-005 is now resolvable: a single journalctl?unit=ict-trader-live&since=<recent-boot-iso>&lines=2000 pull surfaces the set_leverage trace.
